# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y build-essential \
    && npm install -g yarn \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install other dependencies
RUN yarn add @babel/cli @babel/core @babel/preset-env @babel/polyfill @babel/register babel-loader css-loader html-webpack-plugin mini-css-extract-plugin style-loader webpack webpack-cli

# Set the working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app
COPY . /app

# Run npm install to get all dependencies
RUN npm install

# Expose ports 80 and 443
EXPOSE 80 443

# Run the application with nodemon
CMD ["nodemon", "src/index.js"]
